content.text:
1	---
     2	name: docx
     3	description: "Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation."
     4	license: Proprietary. LICENSE.txt has complete terms
     5	---
     6	
     7	# DOCX creation, editing, and analysis
     8	
     9	## Overview
    10	
    11	A .docx file is a ZIP archive containing XML files.
    12	
    13	## Quick Reference
    14	
    15	| Task | Approach |
    16	|------|----------|
    17	| Read/analyze content | `pandoc` or unpack for raw XML |
    18	| Create new document | Use `docx-js` - see Creating New Documents below |
    19	| Edit existing document | Unpack → edit XML → repack - see Editing Existing Documents below |
    20	
    21	### Converting .doc to .docx
    22	
    23	Legacy `.doc` files must be converted before editing:
    24	
    25	```bash
    26	python scripts/office/soffice.py --headless --convert-to docx document.doc
    27	```
    28	
    29	### Reading Content
    30	
    31	```bash
    32	# Text extraction with tracked changes
    33	pandoc --track-changes=all document.docx -o output.md
    34	
    35	# Raw XML access
    36	python scripts/office/unpack.py document.docx unpacked/
    37	```
    38	
    39	### Converting to Images
    40	
    41	```bash
    42	python scripts/office/soffice.py --headless --convert-to pdf document.docx
    43	pdftoppm -jpeg -r 150 document.pdf page
    44	```
    45	
    46	### Accepting Tracked Changes
    47	
    48	To produce a clean document with all tracked changes accepted (requires LibreOffice):
    49	
    50	```bash
    51	python scripts/accept_changes.py input.docx output.docx
    52	```
    53	
    54	---
    55	
    56	## Creating New Documents
    57	
    58	Generate .docx files with JavaScript, then validate. Install: `npm install -g docx`
    59	
    60	### Setup
    61	```javascript
    62	const { Document, Packer, Paragraph, TextRun, Table, TableRow, TableCell, ImageRun,
    63	        Header, Footer, AlignmentType, PageOrientation, LevelFormat, ExternalHyperlink,
    64	        InternalHyperlink, Bookmark, FootnoteReferenceRun, PositionalTab,
    65	        PositionalTabAlignment, PositionalTabRelativeTo, PositionalTabLeader,
    66	        TabStopType, TabStopPosition, Column, SectionType,
    67	        TableOfContents, HeadingLevel, BorderStyle, WidthType, ShadingType,
    68	        VerticalAlign, PageNumber, PageBreak } = require('docx');
    69	
    70	const doc = new Document({ sections: [{ children: [/* content */] }] });
    71	Packer.toBuffer(doc).then(buffer => fs.writeFileSync("doc.docx", buffer));
    72	```
    73	
    74	### Validation
    75	After creating the file, validate it. If validation fails, unpack, fix the XML, and repack.
    76	```bash
    77	python scripts/office/validate.py doc.docx
    78	```
    79	
    80	### Page Size
    81	
    82	```javascript
    83	// CRITICAL: docx-js defaults to A4, not US Letter
    84	// Always set page size explicitly for consistent results
    85	sections: [{
    86	  properties: {
    87	    page: {
    88	      size: {
    89	        width: 12240,   // 8.5 inches in DXA
    90	        height: 15840   // 11 inches in DXA
    91	      },
    92	      margin: { top: 1440, right: 1440, bottom: 1440, left: 1440 } // 1 inch margins
    93	    }
    94	  },
    95	  children: [/* content */]
    96	}]
    97	```
    98	
    99	**Common page sizes (DXA units, 1440 DXA = 1 inch):**
   100	
   101	| Paper | Width | Height | Content Width (1" margins) |
   102	|-------|-------|--------|---------------------------|
   103	| US Letter | 12,240 | 15,840 | 9,360 |
   104	| A4 (default) | 11,906 | 16,838 | 9,026 |
   105	
   106	**Landscape orientation:** docx-js swaps width/height internally, so pass portrait dimensions and let it handle the swap:
   107	```javascript
   108	size: {
   109	  width: 12240,   // Pass SHORT edge as width
   110	  height: 15840,  // Pass LONG edge as height
   111	  orientation: PageOrientation.LANDSCAPE  // docx-js swaps them in the XML
   112	},
   113	// Content width = 15840 - left margin - right margin (uses the long edge)
   114	```
   115	
   116	### Styles (Override Built-in Headings)
   117	
   118	Use Arial as the default font (universally supported). Keep titles black for readability.
   119	
   120	```javascript
   121	const doc = new Document({
   122	  styles: {
   123	    default: { document: { run: { font: "Arial", size: 24 } } }, // 12pt default
   124	    paragraphStyles: [
   125	      // IMPORTANT: Use exact IDs to override built-in styles
   126	      { id: "Heading1", name: "Heading 1", basedOn: "Normal", next: "Normal", quickFormat: true,
   127	        run: { size: 32, bold: true, font: "Arial" },
   128	        paragraph: { spacing: { before: 240, after: 240 }, outlineLevel: 0 } }, // outlineLevel required for TOC
   129	      { id: "Heading2", name: "Heading 2", basedOn: "Normal", next: "Normal", quickFormat: true,
   130	        run: { size: 28, bold: true, font: "Arial" },
   131	        paragraph: { spacing: { before: 180, after: 180 }, outlineLevel: 1 } },
   132	    ]
   133	  },
   134	  sections: [{
   135	    children: [
   136	      new Paragraph({ heading: HeadingLevel.HEADING_1, children: [new TextRun("Title")] }),
   137	    ]
   138	  }]
   139	});
   140	```
   141	
   142	### Lists (NEVER use unicode bullets)
   143	
   144	```javascript
   145	// ❌ WRONG - never manually insert bullet characters
   146	new Paragraph({ children: [new TextRun("• Item")] })  // BAD
   147	new Paragraph({ children: [new TextRun("\u2022 Item")] })  // BAD
   148	
   149	// ✅ CORRECT - use numbering config with LevelFormat.BULLET
   150	const doc = new Document({
   151	  numbering: {
   152	    config: [
   153	      { reference: "bullets",
   154	        levels: [{ level: 0, format: LevelFormat.BULLET, text: "•", alignment: AlignmentType.LEFT,
   155	          style: { paragraph: { indent: { left: 720, hanging: 360 } } } }] },
   156	      { reference: "numbers",
   157	        levels: [{ level: 0, format: LevelFormat.DECIMAL, text: "%1.", alignment: AlignmentType.LEFT,
   158	          style: { paragraph: { indent: { left: 720, hanging: 360 } } } }] },
   159	    ]
   160	  },
   161	  sections: [{
   162	    children: [
   163	      new Paragraph({ numbering: { reference: "bullets", level: 0 },
   164	        children: [new TextRun("Bullet item")] }),
   165	      new Paragraph({ numbering: { reference: "numbers", level: 0 },
   166	        children: [new TextRun("Numbered item")] }),
   167	    ]
   168	  }]
   169	});
   170	
   171	// ⚠️ Each reference creates INDEPENDENT numbering
   172	// Same reference = continues (1,2,3 then 4,5,6)
   173	// Different reference = restarts (1,2,3 then 1,2,3)
   174	```
   175	
   176	### Tables
   177	
   178	**CRITICAL: Tables need dual widths** - set both `columnWidths` on the table AND `width` on each cell. Without both, tables render incorrectly on some platforms.
   179	
   180	```javascript
   181	// CRITICAL: Always set table width for consistent rendering
   182	// CRITICAL: Use ShadingType.CLEAR (not SOLID) to prevent black backgrounds
   183	const border = { style: BorderStyle.SINGLE, size: 1, color: "CCCCCC" };
   184	const borders = { top: border, bottom: border, left: border, right: border };
   185	
   186	new Table({
   187	  width: { size: 9360, type: WidthType.DXA }, // Always use DXA (percentages break in Google Docs)
   188	  columnWidths: [4680, 4680], // Must sum to table width (DXA: 1440 = 1 inch)
   189	  rows: [
   190	    new TableRow({
   191	      children: [
   192	        new TableCell({
   193	          borders,
   194	          width: { size: 4680, type: WidthType.DXA }, // Also set on each cell
   195	          shading: { fill: "D5E8F0", type: ShadingType.CLEAR }, // CLEAR not SOLID
   196	          margins: { top: 80, bottom: 80, left: 120, right: 120 }, // Cell padding (internal, not added to width)
   197	          children: [new Paragraph({ children: [new TextRun("Cell")] })]
   198	        })
   199	      ]
   200	    })
   201	  ]
   202	})
   203	```
   204	
   205	**Table width calculation:**
   206	
   207	Always use `WidthType.DXA` — `WidthType.PERCENTAGE` breaks in Google Docs.
   208	
   209	```javascript
   210	// Table width = sum of columnWidths = content width
   211	// US Letter with 1" margins: 12240 - 2880 = 9360 DXA
   212	width: { size: 9360, type: WidthType.DXA },
   213	columnWidths: [7000, 2360]  // Must sum to table width
   214	```
   215	
   216	**Width rules:**
	< truncated lines 217-374 >
   375	}]
   376	```
   377	
   378	### Critical Rules for docx-js
   379	
   380	- **Set page size explicitly** - docx-js defaults to A4; use US Letter (12240 x 15840 DXA) for US documents
   381	- **Landscape: pass portrait dimensions** - docx-js swaps width/height internally; pass short edge as `width`, long edge as `height`, and set `orientation: PageOrientation.LANDSCAPE`
   382	- **Never use `\n`** - use separate Paragraph elements
   383	- **Never use unicode bullets** - use `LevelFormat.BULLET` with numbering config
   384	- **PageBreak must be in Paragraph** - standalone creates invalid XML
   385	- **ImageRun requires `type`** - always specify png/jpg/etc
   386	- **Always set table `width` with DXA** - never use `WidthType.PERCENTAGE` (breaks in Google Docs)
   387	- **Tables need dual widths** - `columnWidths` array AND cell `width`, both must match
   388	- **Table width = sum of columnWidths** - for DXA, ensure they add up exactly
   389	- **Always add cell margins** - use `margins: { top: 80, bottom: 80, left: 120, right: 120 }` for readable padding
   390	- **Use `ShadingType.CLEAR`** - never SOLID for table shading
   391	- **Never use tables as dividers/rules** - cells have minimum height and render as empty boxes (including in headers/footers); use `border: { bottom: { style: BorderStyle.SINGLE, size: 6, color: "2E75B6", space: 1 } }` on a Paragraph instead. For two-column footers, use tab stops (see Tab Stops section), not tables
   392	- **TOC requires HeadingLevel only** - no custom styles on heading paragraphs
   393	- **Override built-in styles** - use exact IDs: "Heading1", "Heading2", etc.
   394	- **Include `outlineLevel`** - required for TOC (0 for H1, 1 for H2, etc.)
   395	
   396	---
   397	
   398	## Editing Existing Documents
   399	
   400	**Follow all 3 steps in order.**
   401	
   402	### Step 1: Unpack
   403	```bash
   404	python scripts/office/unpack.py document.docx unpacked/
   405	```
   406	Extracts XML, pretty-prints, merges adjacent runs, and converts smart quotes to XML entities (`“` etc.) so they survive editing. Use `--merge-runs false` to skip run merging.
   407	
   408	### Step 2: Edit XML
   409	
   410	Edit files in `unpacked/word/`. See XML Reference below for patterns.
   411	
   412	**Use "Claude" as the author** for tracked changes and comments, unless the user explicitly requests use of a different name.
   413	
   414	**Use the Edit tool directly for string replacement. Do not write Python scripts.** Scripts introduce unnecessary complexity. The Edit tool shows exactly what is being replaced.
   415	
   416	**CRITICAL: Use smart quotes for new content.** When adding text with apostrophes or quotes, use XML entities to produce smart quotes:
   417	```xml
   418	<!-- Use these entities for professional typography -->
   419	<w:t>Here’s a quote: “Hello”</w:t>
   420	```
   421	| Entity | Character |
   422	|--------|-----------|
   423	| `‘` | ‘ (left single) |
   424	| `’` | ’ (right single / apostrophe) |
   425	| `“` | “ (left double) |
   426	| `”` | ” (right double) |
   427	
   428	**Adding comments:** Use `comment.py` to handle boilerplate across multiple XML files (text must be pre-escaped XML):
   429	```bash
   430	python scripts/comment.py unpacked/ 0 "Comment text with & and ’"
   431	python scripts/comment.py unpacked/ 1 "Reply text" --parent 0  # reply to comment 0
   432	python scripts/comment.py unpacked/ 0 "Text" --author "Custom Author"  # custom author name
   433	```
   434	Then add markers to document.xml (see Comments in XML Reference).
   435	
   436	### Step 3: Pack
   437	```bash
   438	python scripts/office/pack.py unpacked/ output.docx --original document.docx
   439	```
   440	Validates with auto-repair, condenses XML, and creates DOCX. Use `--validate false` to skip.
   441	
   442	**Auto-repair will fix:**
   443	- `durableId` >= 0x7FFFFFFF (regenerates valid ID)
   444	- Missing `xml:space="preserve"` on `<w:t>` with whitespace
   445	
   446	**Auto-repair won't fix:**
   447	- Malformed XML, invalid element nesting, missing relationships, schema violations
   448	
   449	### Common Pitfalls
   450	
   451	- **Replace entire `<w:r>` elements**: When adding tracked changes, replace the whole `<w:r>...</w:r>` block with `<w:del>...<w:ins>...` as siblings. Don't inject tracked change tags inside a run.
   452	- **Preserve `<w:rPr>` formatting**: Copy the original run's `<w:rPr>` block into your tracked change runs to maintain bold, font size, etc.
   453	
   454	---
   455	
   456	## XML Reference
   457	
   458	### Schema Compliance
   459	
   460	- **Element order in `<w:pPr>`**: `<w:pStyle>`, `<w:numPr>`, `<w:spacing>`, `<w:ind>`, `<w:jc>`, `<w:rPr>` last
   461	- **Whitespace**: Add `xml:space="preserve"` to `<w:t>` with leading/trailing spaces
   462	- **RSIDs**: Must be 8-digit hex (e.g., `00AB1234`)
   463	
   464	### Tracked Changes
   465	
   466	**Insertion:**
   467	```xml
   468	<w:ins w:id="1" w:author="Claude" w:date="2025-01-01T00:00:00Z">
   469	  <w:r><w:t>inserted text</w:t></w:r>
   470	</w:ins>
   471	```
   472	
   473	**Deletion:**
   474	```xml
   475	<w:del w:id="2" w:author="Claude" w:date="2025-01-01T00:00:00Z">
   476	  <w:r><w:delText>deleted text</w:delText></w:r>
   477	</w:del>
   478	```
   479	
   480	**Inside `<w:del>`**: Use `<w:delText>` instead of `<w:t>`, and `<w:delInstrText>` instead of `<w:instrText>`.
   481	
   482	**Minimal edits** - only mark what changes:
   483	```xml
   484	<!-- Change "30 days" to "60 days" -->
   485	<w:r><w:t>The term is </w:t></w:r>
   486	<w:del w:id="1" w:author="Claude" w:date="...">
   487	  <w:r><w:delText>30</w:delText></w:r>
   488	</w:del>
   489	<w:ins w:id="2" w:author="Claude" w:date="...">
   490	  <w:r><w:t>60</w:t></w:r>
   491	</w:ins>
   492	<w:r><w:t> days.</w:t></w:r>
   493	```
   494	
   495	**Deleting entire paragraphs/list items** - when removing ALL content from a paragraph, also mark the paragraph mark as deleted so it merges with the next paragraph. Add `<w:del/>` inside `<w:pPr><w:rPr>`:
   496	```xml
   497	<w:p>
   498	  <w:pPr>
   499	    <w:numPr>...</w:numPr>  <!-- list numbering if present -->
   500	    <w:rPr>
   501	      <w:del w:id="1" w:author="Claude" w:date="2025-01-01T00:00:00Z"/>
   502	    </w:rPr>
   503	  </w:pPr>
   504	  <w:del w:id="2" w:author="Claude" w:date="2025-01-01T00:00:00Z">
   505	    <w:r><w:delText>Entire paragraph content being deleted...</w:delText></w:r>
   506	  </w:del>
   507	</w:p>
   508	```
   509	Without the `<w:del/>` in `<w:pPr><w:rPr>`, accepting changes leaves an empty paragraph/list item.
   510	
   511	**Rejecting another author's insertion** - nest deletion inside their insertion:
   512	```xml
   513	<w:ins w:author="Jane" w:id="5">
   514	  <w:del w:author="Claude" w:id="10">
   515	    <w:r><w:delText>their inserted text</w:delText></w:r>
   516	  </w:del>
   517	</w:ins>
   518	```
   519	
   520	**Restoring another author's deletion** - add insertion after (don't modify their deletion):
   521	```xml
   522	<w:del w:author="Jane" w:id="5">
   523	  <w:r><w:delText>deleted text</w:delText></w:r>
   524	</w:del>
   525	<w:ins w:author="Claude" w:id="10">
   526	  <w:r><w:t>deleted text</w:t></w:r>
   527	</w:ins>
   528	```
   529	
   530	### Comments
   531	
   532	After running `comment.py` (see Step 2), add markers to document.xml. For replies, use `--parent` flag and nest markers inside the parent's.
   533	
   534	**CRITICAL: `<w:commentRangeStart>` and `<w:commentRangeEnd>` are siblings of `<w:r>`, never inside `<w:r>`.**
   535	
   536	```xml
   537	<!-- Comment markers are direct children of w:p, never inside w:r -->
   538	<w:commentRangeStart w:id="0"/>
   539	<w:del w:id="1" w:author="Claude" w:date="2025-01-01T00:00:00Z">
   540	  <w:r><w:delText>deleted</w:delText></w:r>
   541	</w:del>
   542	<w:r><w:t> more text</w:t></w:r>
   543	<w:commentRangeEnd w:id="0"/>
   544	<w:r><w:rPr><w:rStyle w:val="CommentReference"/></w:rPr><w:commentReference w:id="0"/></w:r>
   545	
   546	<!-- Comment 0 with reply 1 nested inside -->
   547	<w:commentRangeStart w:id="0"/>
   548	  <w:commentRangeStart w:id="1"/>
   549	  <w:r><w:t>text</w:t></w:r>
   550	  <w:commentRangeEnd w:id="1"/>
   551	<w:commentRangeEnd w:id="0"/>
   552	<w:r><w:rPr><w:rStyle w:val="CommentReference"/></w:rPr><w:commentReference w:id="0"/></w:r>
   553	<w:r><w:rPr><w:rStyle w:val="CommentReference"/></w:rPr><w:commentReference w:id="1"/></w:r>
   554	```
   555	
   556	### Images
   557	
   558	1. Add image file to `word/media/`
   559	2. Add relationship to `word/_rels/document.xml.rels`:
   560	```xml
   561	<Relationship Id="rId5" Type=".../image" Target="media/image1.png"/>
   562	```
   563	3. Add content type to `[Content_Types].xml`:
   564	```xml
   565	<Default Extension="png" ContentType="image/png"/>
   566	```
   567	4. Reference in document.xml:
   568	```xml
   569	<w:drawing>
   570	  <wp:inline>
   571	    <wp:extent cx="914400" cy="914400"/>  <!-- EMUs: 914400 = 1 inch -->
   572	    <a:graphic>
   573	      <a:graphicData uri=".../picture">
   574	        <pic:pic>
   575	          <pic:blipFill><a:blip r:embed="rId5"/></pic:blipFill>
   576	        </pic:pic>
   577	      </a:graphicData>
   578	    </a:graphic>
   579	  </wp:inline>
   580	</w:drawing>
   581	```
   582	
   583	---
   584	
   585	## Dependencies
   586	
   587	- **pandoc**: Text extraction
   588	- **docx**: `npm install -g docx` (new documents)
   589	- **LibreOffice**: PDF conversion (auto-configured for sandboxed environments via `scripts/office/soffice.py`)
   590	- **Poppler**: `pdftoppm` for images
   591
Actually, since you work in markdown and Obsidian, a `.md` spec will be more useful than a `.docx`. Let me write this directly.

input.description: MVP spec for Project Aegis - Daily Intelligence Note
input.file_text:
# Project Aegis — MVP Spec: Daily Intelligence Note

## Objective

Every morning, produce a single markdown file `Daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md` containing:

1. **News Briefing** — AI industry, Anthropic, Boris Cherny (Claude insights/tweets)
2. **Calendar** — Today's events from Google Calendar
3. **Tasks** — Today's todo list (vault-native, markdown-based)
4. **Training** — Summary pulled from existing training plan context (read-only)

---

## System Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  VPS (automated)                 │
│                                                  │
│  cron (6:00 AM) ─► fetch_all.py                 │
│                      ├── news_fetcher.py         │
│                      ├── calendar_fetcher.py     │
│                      └── writes to .system/state/│
│                                                  │
│  Syncthing ◄──────────────────────► Laptop/Phone │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              Laptop (user-triggered)             │
│                                                  │
│  start_day ─► context_builder.py                 │
│                  reads .system/state/*            │
│                  reads data/tasks/               │
│                  reads data/training/             │
│                  writes .system/context/          │
│                                                  │
│              ─► agent_daily_planner.py            │
│                  reads .system/context/*          │
│                  writes Daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Vault Structure (MVP subset)

```
vault/
  Daily/
    2025-06-15.md              ← generated daily note

  data/
    tasks/
      inbox.md                 ← quick capture
      today.md                 ← today's tasks (manual + generated)
      backlog.md               ← someday/maybe
    training/
      plan.md                  ← current training plan (maintained separately)
      log.md                   ← recent training log

  .system/
    state/
      calendar_state.json      ← fetched from Google Calendar
      news_state.json          ← fetched from news sources
    context/
      calendar_context.md      ← human-readable calendar summary
      news_context.md          ← human-readable news briefing
      tasks_context.md         ← assembled from data/tasks/
      training_context.md      ← assembled from data/training/
      daily_context.md         ← merged context for agent
    config/
      interests.json           ← news topics and sources config
    logs/
      fetch.log                ← ingestion logs
      agent.log                ← agent run logs
```

---

## Component Specs

### 1. News Fetcher (`scripts/fetch/news_fetcher.py`)

**Responsibility:** Fetch news matching configured interests, write structured state.

**Interface:**

```python
class NewsFetcher:
    """Abstract base. Swap implementations without changing the pipeline."""

    def fetch(self, interests: list[str]) -> list[NewsItem]:
        raise NotImplementedError

@dataclass
class NewsItem:
    title: str
    source: str          # e.g. "twitter", "rss", "newsapi"
    url: str
    summary: str         # 1-3 sentences
    topic: str           # matched interest tag
    published: datetime
    relevance: float     # 0-1, source-specific scoring
```

**Config** (`.system/config/interests.json`):

```json
{
  "topics": [
    {
      "name": "AI Industry",
      "keywords": ["artificial intelligence", "LLM", "foundation model"],
      "sources": ["rss", "newsapi"]
    },
    {
      "name": "Anthropic",
      "keywords": ["anthropic", "claude", "claude code"],
      "sources": ["rss", "twitter", "newsapi"]
    },
    {
      "name": "Boris Cherny",
      "keywords": ["boris cherny"],
      "sources": ["twitter"],
      "twitter_handles": ["borischerny"]
    }
  ],
  "max_items_per_topic": 5,
  "max_age_hours": 24
}
```

**Output** (`.system/state/news_state.json`):

```json
{
  "fetched_at": "2025-06-15T06:00:00Z",
  "items": [
    {
      "title": "Anthropic releases Claude 4",
      "source": "rss",
      "url": "https://...",
      "summary": "Anthropic announced...",
      "topic": "Anthropic",
      "published": "2025-06-14T18:30:00Z",
      "relevance": 0.95
    }
  ],
  "errors": []
}
```

**Implementation note:** The fetcher interface is deliberately abstract. For MVP, start with whichever source is easiest to set up (likely RSS via `feedparser` or a news API). Add Twitter/X scraping later. The pipeline doesn't care — it reads `news_state.json` regardless of how it was populated.

---

### 2. Calendar Fetcher (`scripts/fetch/calendar_fetcher.py`)

**Responsibility:** Fetch today's + tomorrow's events from Google Calendar API.

**Output** (`.system/state/calendar_state.json`):

```json
{
  "fetched_at": "2025-06-15T06:00:00Z",
  "date": "2025-06-15",
  "events": [
    {
      "title": "Team standup",
      "start": "2025-06-15T09:00:00+03:00",
      "end": "2025-06-15T09:30:00+03:00",
      "location": "Google Meet",
      "calendar": "Work",
      "all_day": false
    }
  ],
  "tomorrow_preview": [
    {
      "title": "Dentist",
      "start": "2025-06-16T14:00:00+03:00",
      "all_day": false
    }
  ]
}
```

**Auth:** Google Calendar API with OAuth2 service account or saved refresh token. Store credentials in `.system/config/google_credentials.json` (gitignored).

---

### 3. Task System

**No fetcher needed.** Tasks are vault-native markdown files.

**Format** (`data/tasks/today.md`):

```markdown
# Today

- [ ] Review PR for auth module
- [ ] Send status update to team
- [x] Fix calendar sync bug

# Carried Over
- [ ] Write integration tests (from 2025-06-14)
```

**Format** (`data/tasks/inbox.md`):

```markdown
# Inbox
- [ ] Look into Syncthing conflict resolution
- [ ] Research VPS backup options
```

Tasks are edited by you directly in Obsidian. The context builder reads them as-is. The Daily Planner agent may suggest new tasks (appended to `today.md` marked as `<!-- suggested -->`).

---

### 4. Training Context

**No fetcher needed for MVP.** You maintain `data/training/plan.md` and `data/training/log.md` separately.

The context builder reads these files and produces a short training summary. The agent uses this for awareness only — no training suggestions in MVP.

---

### 5. Context Builder (`scripts/build_context.py`)

**Responsibility:** Transform state files + vault files into context modules, then merge into a single `daily_context.md` for the agent.

**Process:**

```
calendar_state.json  ──► calendar_context.md
news_state.json      ──► news_context.md
data/tasks/*.md      ──► tasks_context.md
data/training/*.md   ──► training_context.md
                          │
                          ▼
                     daily_context.md  (merged)
```

**Context building is deterministic (Python templates, not AI).** This keeps it fast, free, and predictable.

**Example template logic for `calendar_context.md`:**

```python
def build_calendar_context(state: dict) -> str:
    lines = [f"# Calendar — {state['date']}", ""]
    for event in state["events"]:
        time = format_time(event["start"])
        end = format_time(event["end"])
        lines.append(f"- {time}–{end}: {event['title']}")
        if event.get("location"):
            lines.append(f"  Location: {event['location']}")
    if state.get("tomorrow_preview"):
        lines.append("")
        lines.append("## Tomorrow")
        for event in state["tomorrow_preview"]:
            lines.append(f"- {format_time(event['start'])}: {event['title']}")
    return "\n".join(lines)
```

**`daily_context.md` structure:**

```markdown
---
generated: 2025-06-15T06:05:00Z
date: 2025-06-15
weekday: Sunday
---

# Calendar
[calendar_context.md content]

# News Briefing
[news_context.md content]

# Tasks
[tasks_context.md content]

# Training Status
[training_context.md content]
```

**Token budget target:** daily_context.md should stay under ~2000 tokens. The context builder truncates news items if needed (lowest relevance first).

---

### 6. Daily Planner Agent (`scripts/agent_daily_planner.py`)

**Responsibility:** Read `daily_context.md`, produce the daily note.

**Interface (runtime-agnostic):**

```python
class AgentRuntime:
    """Abstract base. Swap Claude API / Claude Code / other."""

    def run(self, system_prompt: str, user_prompt: str) -> str:
        raise NotImplementedError

class ClaudeAPIRuntime(AgentRuntime):
    """Default implementation using Claude API via Python SDK."""

    def run(self, system_prompt: str, user_prompt: str) -> str:
        # anthropic.Anthropic().messages.create(...)
        pass

class ClaudeCodeRuntime(AgentRuntime):
    """Alternative: invoke Claude Code CLI."""

    def run(self, system_prompt: str, user_prompt: str) -> str:
        # subprocess.run(["claude", "-p", ...])
        pass
```

**System prompt:**

```
You are Aegis Daily Planner — a personal intelligence assistant.

You receive a daily context briefing and produce a structured daily note.

Rules:
- Be concise and actionable
- News: highlight 3-5 most important items, one sentence each
- Calendar: list events chronologically
- Tasks: list today's tasks, suggest 1-2 if light day
- Training: brief status only, no coaching
- Tone: direct, no filler
- Output: clean markdown, ready for Obsidian
```

**User prompt:**

```
Today is {date}, {weekday}.

{daily_context.md contents}

Generate my daily note.
```

**Output** (`Daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md`):

```markdown
---
date: 2025-06-15
type: daily
generated: true
---

# Sunday, June 15

## News
- **Anthropic** released Claude 4 with extended context — [link]
- **Boris Cherny** shared a thread on Claude Code workflows for monorepos
- **OpenAI** announced GPT-5 preview access for enterprise
- **EU AI Act** enforcement guidelines published

## Schedule
- 09:00–09:30 — Team standup (Google Meet)
- 14:00–15:00 — Architecture review

**Tomorrow:** Dentist at 14:00

## Tasks
- [ ] Review PR for auth module
- [ ] Send status update to team
- [ ] Write integration tests (carried over)

## Training
Week progress: 20/100km. Long ride needed before Sunday.

---
*Generated by Aegis at 06:05*
```

---

## Orchestration

### `start_day` (user-triggered, laptop)

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# start_day — generate today's daily note

set -e

VAULT="$HOME/vault"
DATE=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
DAILY="$VAULT/Daily/$DATE.md"

if [ -f "$DAILY" ]; then
    echo "Daily note already exists: $DAILY"
    echo "Re-run with --force to regenerate"
    [[ "$1" != "--force" ]] && exit 0
fi

echo "Building context..."
python3 "$VAULT/scripts/build_context.py"

echo "Running Daily Planner agent..."
python3 "$VAULT/scripts/agent_daily_planner.py" > "$DAILY"

echo "Done: $DAILY"
```

### `fetch_all.py` (cron, VPS)

```bash
# crontab entry
0 6 * * * cd /home/user/vault && python3 scripts/fetch/fetch_all.py >> .system/logs/fetch.log 2>&1
```

```python
"""fetch_all.py — run all fetchers, log results."""

import logging
from fetch.news_fetcher import NewsFetcher
from fetch.calendar_fetcher import CalendarFetcher

log = logging.getLogger("aegis.fetch")

def main():
    fetchers = [
        CalendarFetcher(),
        NewsFetcher(),
    ]
    for f in fetchers:
        try:
            f.run()
            log.info(f"{f.__class__.__name__}: OK")
        except Exception as e:
            log.error(f"{f.__class__.__name__}: FAILED — {e}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

---

## File Manifest

```
vault/scripts/
  fetch/
    __init__.py
    fetch_all.py              ← cron entry point
    news_fetcher.py           ← NewsItem + NewsFetcher base + implementation
    calendar_fetcher.py       ← Google Calendar fetch + write state
  build_context.py            ← reads state + vault, writes context modules
  agent_daily_planner.py      ← reads context, calls agent, writes daily note
  runtime.py                  ← AgentRuntime base + implementations
  start_day                   ← bash entry point (user-triggered)
```

---

## Failure Handling

| Failure | Detection | Behavior |
|---------|-----------|----------|
| News fetch fails | `errors` array in news_state.json | Agent runs without news section, log warning |
| Calendar fetch fails | Missing/stale calendar_state.json | Agent notes "calendar unavailable", log warning |
| State files missing | build_context.py checks file age | Skip that context module, warn in daily note |
| Agent call fails | agent_daily_planner.py try/except | Write partial note with available context, log error |
| Syncthing lag | start_day checks state file timestamps | Warn if state is >2 hours old, offer to re-fetch locally |

**Staleness check:**

```python
def is_stale(state_file: Path, max_age_hours: int = 2) -> bool:
    if not state_file.exists():
        return True
    fetched = json.loads(state_file.read_text()).get("fetched_at")
    if not fetched:
        return True
    age = datetime.now(UTC) - datetime.fromisoformat(fetched)
    return age.total_seconds() > max_age_hours * 3600
```

---

## Implementation Order

### Phase 1 — Skeleton (day 1)

- [ ] Create vault folder structure
- [ ] Set up Syncthing between VPS and laptop
- [ ] Create `interests.json` with your three topics
- [ ] Write `start_day` shell script
- [ ] Write `build_context.py` with hardcoded test data
- [ ] Confirm: `start_day` produces a daily note from test context

### Phase 2 — Calendar pipeline (day 2-3)

- [ ] Set up Google Calendar API credentials
- [ ] Implement `calendar_fetcher.py`
- [ ] Implement calendar context template in `build_context.py`
- [ ] Confirm: real calendar data flows to daily note

### Phase 3 — News pipeline (day 3-5)

- [ ] Pick first news source (RSS recommended: Anthropic blog, AI news feeds)
- [ ] Implement `news_fetcher.py` with one concrete source
- [ ] Implement news context template
- [ ] Confirm: news items appear in daily note

### Phase 4 — Agent integration (day 5-6)

- [ ] Implement `runtime.py` with your chosen runtime
- [ ] Write and tune the Daily Planner system prompt
- [ ] Replace raw context dump with agent-generated daily note
- [ ] Iterate on prompt until output quality is right

### Phase 5 — Automation (day 7)

- [ ] Set up cron for `fetch_all.py` on VPS
- [ ] Add staleness checks to `start_day`
- [ ] Add logging
- [ ] Test the full morning loop for 3 days

---

## What This Spec Deliberately Excludes

These are in the full Aegis design but **not in MVP**:

- Voice capture pipeline
- Patch proposal system (no agent writes to critical files yet)
- Knowledge Distiller / weekly summaries
- Project Monitor agent
- Training Coach agent
- Event-driven architecture (MVP uses cron + manual trigger)
- SQLite (JSON files are sufficient at this scale)

Each can be added incrementally once the daily loop is stable.

---

## Decision Log

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| Task storage | Vault-native markdown | No external dependency, edit in Obsidian |
| Context building | Deterministic templates | Free, fast, predictable |
| Agent runtime | Abstract interface | Decide later, swap without rewriting pipeline |
| News sources | Abstract interface | Start with easiest, add sources over time |
| VPS role | Fetch only | Avoids sync conflicts, agent runs where you are |
| Daily note conflict | Check-before-write | Simple, prevents accidental overwrites |
| MVP scope | Daily Planner only | One agent, full pipeline, prove the architecture |

content.message: MVP spec for Project Aegis - Daily Intelligence Note

display_content.json_block:
{"language": "markdown", "code": "# Project Aegis — MVP Spec: Daily Intelligence Note

## Objective

Every morning, produce a single markdown file `Daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md` containing:

1. **News Briefing** — AI industry, Anthropic, Boris Cherny (Claude insights/tweets)
2. **Calendar** — Today's events from Google Calendar
3. **Tasks** — Today's todo list (vault-native, markdown-based)
4. **Training** — Summary pulled from existing training plan context (read-only)

---

## System Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  VPS (automated)                 │
│                                                  │
│  cron (6:00 AM) ─► fetch_all.py                 │
│                      ├── news_fetcher.py         │
│                      ├── calendar_fetcher.py     │
│                      └── writes to .system/state/│
│                                                  │
│  Syncthing ◄──────────────────────► Laptop/Phone │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              Laptop (user-triggered)             │
│                                                  │
│  start_day ─► context_builder.py                 │
│                  reads .system/state/*            │
│                  reads data/tasks/               │
│                  reads data/training/             │
│                  writes .system/context/          │
│                                                  │
│              ─► agent_daily_planner.py            │
│                  reads .system/context/*          │
│                  writes Daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Vault Structure (MVP subset)

```
vault/
  Daily/
    2025-06-15.md              ← generated daily note

  data/
    tasks/
      inbox.md                 ← quick capture
      today.md                 ← today's tasks (manual + generated)
      backlog.md               ← someday/maybe
    training/
      plan.md                  ← current training plan (maintained separately)
      log.md                   ← recent training log

  .system/
    state/
      calendar_state.json      ← fetched from Google Calendar
      news_state.json          ← fetched from news sources
    context/
      calendar_context.md      ← human-readable calendar summary
      news_context.md          ← human-readable news briefing
      tasks_context.md         ← assembled from data/tasks/
      training_context.md      ← assembled from data/training/
      daily_context.md         ← merged context for agent
    config/
      interests.json           ← news topics and sources config
    logs/
      fetch.log                ← ingestion logs
      agent.log                ← agent run logs
```

---

## Component Specs

### 1. News Fetcher (`scripts/fetch/news_fetcher.py`)

**Responsibility:** Fetch news matching configured interests, write structured state.

**Interface:**

```python
class NewsFetcher:
    """Abstract base. Swap implementations without changing the pipeline."""

    def fetch(self, interests: list[str]) -> list[NewsItem]:
        raise NotImplementedError

@dataclass
class NewsItem:
    title: str
    source: str          # e.g. "twitter", "rss", "newsapi"
    url: str
    summary: str         # 1-3 sentences
    topic: str           # matched interest tag
    published: datetime
    relevance: float     # 0-1, source-specific scoring
```

**Config** (`.system/config/interests.json`):

```json
{
  "topics": [
    {
      "name": "AI Industry",
      "keywords": ["artificial intelligence", "LLM", "foundation model"],
      "sources": ["rss", "newsapi"]
    },
    {
      "name": "Anthropic",
      "keywords": ["anthropic", "claude", "claude code"],
      "sources": ["rss", "twitter", "newsapi"]
    },
    {
      "name": "Boris Cherny",
      "keywords": ["boris cherny"],
      "sources": ["twitter"],
      "twitter_handles": ["borischerny"]
    }
  ],
  "max_items_per_topic": 5,
  "max_age_hours": 24
}
```

**Output** (`.system/state/news_state.json`):

```json
{
  "fetched_at": "2025-06-15T06:00:00Z",
  "items": [
    {
      "title": "Anthropic releases Claude 4",
      "source": "rss",
      "url": "https://...",
      "summary": "Anthropic announced...",
      "topic": "Anthropic",
      "published": "2025-06-14T18:30:00Z",
      "relevance": 0.95
    }
  ],
  "errors": []
}
```

**Implementation note:** The fetcher interface is deliberately abstract. For MVP, start with whichever source is easiest to set up (likely RSS via `feedparser` or a news API). Add Twitter/X scraping later. The pipeline doesn't care — it reads `news_state.json` regardless of how it was populated.

---

### 2. Calendar Fetcher (`scripts/fetch/calendar_fetcher.py`)

**Responsibility:** Fetch today's + tomorrow's events from Google Calendar API.

**Output** (`.system/state/calendar_state.json`):

```json
{
  "fetched_at": "2025-06-15T06:00:00Z",
  "date": "2025-06-15",
  "events": [
    {
      "title": "Team standup",
      "start": "2025-06-15T09:00:00+03:00",
      "end": "2025-06-15T09:30:00+03:00",
      "location": "Google Meet",
      "calendar": "Work",
      "all_day": false
    }
  ],
  "tomorrow_preview": [
    {
      "title": "Dentist",
      "start": "2025-06-16T14:00:00+03:00",
      "all_day": false
    }
  ]
}
```

**Auth:** Google Calendar API with OAuth2 service account or saved refresh token. Store credentials in `.system/config/google_credentials.json` (gitignored).

---

### 3. Task System

**No fetcher needed.** Tasks are vault-native markdown files.

**Format** (`data/tasks/today.md`):

```markdown
# Today

- [ ] Review PR for auth module
- [ ] Send status update to team
- [x] Fix calendar sync bug

# Carried Over
- [ ] Write integration tests (from 2025-06-14)
```

**Format** (`data/tasks/inbox.md`):

```markdown
# Inbox
- [ ] Look into Syncthing conflict resolution
- [ ] Research VPS backup options
```

Tasks are edited by you directly in Obsidian. The context builder reads them as-is. The Daily Planner agent may suggest new tasks (appended to `today.md` marked as `<!-- suggested -->`).

---

### 4. Training Context

**No fetcher needed for MVP.** You maintain `data/training/plan.md` and `data/training/log.md` separately.

The context builder reads these files and produces a short training summary. The agent uses this for awareness only — no training suggestions in MVP.

---

### 5. Context Builder (`scripts/build_context.py`)

**Responsibility:** Transform state files + vault files into context modules, then merge into a single `daily_context.md` for the agent.

**Process:**

```
calendar_state.json  ──► calendar_context.md
news_state.json      ──► news_context.md
data/tasks/*.md      ──► tasks_context.md
data/training/*.md   ──► training_context.md
                          │
                          ▼
                     daily_context.md  (merged)
```

**Context building is deterministic (Python templates, not AI).** This keeps it fast, free, and predictable.

**Example template logic for `calendar_context.md`:**

```python
def build_calendar_context(state: dict) -> str:
    lines = [f"# Calendar — {state['date']}", ""]
    for event in state["events"]:
        time = format_time(event["start"])
        end = format_time(event["end"])
        lines.append(f"- {time}–{end}: {event['title']}")
        if event.get("location"):
            lines.append(f"  Location: {event['location']}")
    if state.get("tomorrow_preview"):
        lines.append("")
        lines.append("## Tomorrow")
        for event in state["tomorrow_preview"]:
            lines.append(f"- {format_time(event['start'])}: {event['title']}")
    return "\n".join(lines)
```

**`daily_context.md` structure:**

```markdown
---
generated: 2025-06-15T06:05:00Z
date: 2025-06-15
weekday: Sunday
---

# Calendar
[calendar_context.md content]

# News Briefing
[news_context.md content]

# Tasks
[tasks_context.md content]

# Training Status
[training_context.md content]
```

**Token budget target:** daily_context.md should stay under ~2000 tokens. The context builder truncates news items if needed (lowest relevance first).

---

### 6. Daily Planner Agent (`scripts/agent_daily_planner.py`)

**Responsibility:** Read `daily_context.md`, produce the daily note.

**Interface (runtime-agnostic):**

```python
class AgentRuntime:
    """Abstract base. Swap Claude API / Claude Code / other."""

    def run(self, system_prompt: str, user_prompt: str) -> str:
        raise NotImplementedError

class ClaudeAPIRuntime(AgentRuntime):
    """Default implementation using Claude API via Python SDK."""

    def run(self, system_prompt: str, user_prompt: str) -> str:
        # anthropic.Anthropic().messages.create(...)
        pass

class ClaudeCodeRuntime(AgentRuntime):
    """Alternative: invoke Claude Code CLI."""

    def run(self, system_prompt: str, user_prompt: str) -> str:
        # subprocess.run(["claude", "-p", ...])
        pass
```

**System prompt:**

```
You are Aegis Daily Planner — a personal intelligence assistant.

You receive a daily context briefing and produce a structured daily note.

Rules:
- Be concise and actionable
- News: highlight 3-5 most important items, one sentence each
- Calendar: list events chronologically
- Tasks: list today's tasks, suggest 1-2 if light day
- Training: brief status only, no coaching
- Tone: direct, no filler
- Output: clean markdown, ready for Obsidian
```

**User prompt:**

```
Today is {date}, {weekday}.

{daily_context.md contents}

Generate my daily note.
```

**Output** (`Daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md`):

```markdown
---
date: 2025-06-15
type: daily
generated: true
---

# Sunday, June 15

## News
- **Anthropic** released Claude 4 with extended context — [link]
- **Boris Cherny** shared a thread on Claude Code workflows for monorepos
- **OpenAI** announced GPT-5 preview access for enterprise
- **EU AI Act** enforcement guidelines published

## Schedule
- 09:00–09:30 — Team standup (Google Meet)
- 14:00–15:00 — Architecture review

**Tomorrow:** Dentist at 14:00

## Tasks
- [ ] Review PR for auth module
- [ ] Send status update to team
- [ ] Write integration tests (carried over)

## Training
Week progress: 20/100km. Long ride needed before Sunday.

---
*Generated by Aegis at 06:05*
```

---

## Orchestration

### `start_day` (user-triggered, laptop)

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# start_day — generate today's daily note

set -e

VAULT="$HOME/vault"
DATE=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
DAILY="$VAULT/Daily/$DATE.md"

if [ -f "$DAILY" ]; then
    echo "Daily note already exists: $DAILY"
    echo "Re-run with --force to regenerate"
    [[ "$1" != "--force" ]] && exit 0
fi

echo "Building context..."
python3 "$VAULT/scripts/build_context.py"

echo "Running Daily Planner agent..."
python3 "$VAULT/scripts/agent_daily_planner.py" > "$DAILY"

echo "Done: $DAILY"
```

### `fetch_all.py` (cron, VPS)

```bash
# crontab entry
0 6 * * * cd /home/user/vault && python3 scripts/fetch/fetch_all.py >> .system/logs/fetch.log 2>&1
```

```python
"""fetch_all.py — run all fetchers, log results."""

import logging
from fetch.news_fetcher import NewsFetcher
from fetch.calendar_fetcher import CalendarFetcher

log = logging.getLogger("aegis.fetch")

def main():
    fetchers = [
        CalendarFetcher(),
        NewsFetcher(),
    ]
    for f in fetchers:
        try:
            f.run()
            log.info(f"{f.__class__.__name__}: OK")
        except Exception as e:
            log.error(f"{f.__class__.__name__}: FAILED — {e}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

---

## File Manifest

```
vault/scripts/
  fetch/
    __init__.py
    fetch_all.py              ← cron entry point
    news_fetcher.py           ← NewsItem + NewsFetcher base + implementation
    calendar_fetcher.py       ← Google Calendar fetch + write state
  build_context.py            ← reads state + vault, writes context modules
  agent_daily_planner.py      ← reads context, calls agent, writes daily note
  runtime.py                  ← AgentRuntime base + implementations
  start_day                   ← bash entry point (user-triggered)
```

---

## Failure Handling

| Failure | Detection | Behavior |
|---------|-----------|----------|
| News fetch fails | `errors` array in news_state.json | Agent runs without news section, log warning |
| Calendar fetch fails | Missing/stale calendar_state.json | Agent notes "calendar unavailable", log warning |
| State files missing | build_context.py checks file age | Skip that context module, warn in daily note |
| Agent call fails | agent_daily_planner.py try/except | Write partial note with available context, log error |
| Syncthing lag | start_day checks state file timestamps | Warn if state is >2 hours old, offer to re-fetch locally |

**Staleness check:**

```python
def is_stale(state_file: Path, max_age_hours: int = 2) -> bool:
    if not state_file.exists():
        return True
    fetched = json.loads(state_file.read_text()).get("fetched_at")
    if not fetched:
        return True
    age = datetime.now(UTC) - datetime.fromisoformat(fetched)
    return age.total_seconds() > max_age_hours * 3600
```

---

## Implementation Order

### Phase 1 — Skeleton (day 1)

- [ ] Create vault folder structure
- [ ] Set up Syncthing between VPS and laptop
- [ ] Create `interests.json` with your three topics
- [ ] Write `start_day` shell script
- [ ] Write `build_context.py` with hardcoded test data
- [ ] Confirm: `start_day` produces a daily note from test context

### Phase 2 — Calendar pipeline (day 2-3)

- [ ] Set up Google Calendar API credentials
- [ ] Implement `calendar_fetcher.py`
- [ ] Implement calendar context template in `build_context.py`
- [ ] Confirm: real calendar data flows to daily note

### Phase 3 — News pipeline (day 3-5)

- [ ] Pick first news source (RSS recommended: Anthropic blog, AI news feeds)
- [ ] Implement `news_fetcher.py` with one concrete source
- [ ] Implement news context template
- [ ] Confirm: news items appear in daily note

### Phase 4 — Agent integration (day 5-6)

- [ ] Implement `runtime.py` with your chosen runtime
- [ ] Write and tune the Daily Planner system prompt
- [ ] Replace raw context dump with agent-generated daily note
- [ ] Iterate on prompt until output quality is right

### Phase 5 — Automation (day 7)

- [ ] Set up cron for `fetch_all.py` on VPS
- [ ] Add staleness checks to `start_day`
- [ ] Add logging
- [ ] Test the full morning loop for 3 days

---

## What This Spec Deliberately Excludes

These are in the full Aegis design but **not in MVP**:

- Voice capture pipeline
- Patch proposal system (no agent writes to critical files yet)
- Knowledge Distiller / weekly summaries
- Project Monitor agent
- Training Coach agent
- Event-driven architecture (MVP uses cron + manual trigger)
- SQLite (JSON files are sufficient at this scale)

Each can be added incrementally once the daily loop is stable.

---

## Decision Log

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| Task storage | Vault-native markdown | No external dependency, edit in Obsidian |
| Context building | Deterministic templates | Free, fast, predictable |
| Agent runtime | Abstract interface | Decide later, swap without rewriting pipeline |
| News sources | Abstract interface | Start with easiest, add sources over time |
| VPS role | Fetch only | Avoids sync conflicts, agent runs where you are |
| Daily note conflict | Check-before-write | Simple, prevents accidental overwrites |
| MVP scope | Daily Planner only | One agent, full pipeline, prove the architecture |

content.text:
Here's your MVP spec. The key architectural decisions:

**VPS fetches, laptop reasons.** The VPS runs cron at 6 AM to pull news and calendar into JSON state files. Syncthing moves them to your laptop. You trigger `start_day` when you're ready — the agent runs locally with fresh context.

**Everything is pluggable.** News sources, agent runtime (API vs Claude Code), and even the task format are all behind abstract interfaces. You don't need to decide on any of those today — pick the easiest option for each and swap later.

**Context building is deterministic, not AI.** Python templates turn JSON state into markdown context. The agent only touches the final reasoning step. This keeps token costs minimal and behavior predictable.

**Implementation is ~7 days** if you go sequentially: skeleton → calendar pipeline → news pipeline → agent → automation. Phase 1 can be done in an evening with hardcoded test data, and you'll have a working `start_day` before you write a single fetcher.

The one thing I'd flag — once you start Phase 2 (Google Calendar API), that's where the most annoying setup work lives (OAuth, credentials, refresh tokens). Budget extra time there.

chat_messages.text:
You're right — testing is non-negotiable for something you'll iterate on for months. And the spec as-is is honest about what it excludes but doesn't address how the architecture handles growth. Let me give you a straight assessment.

## What the spec gets right for kickoff

The pipeline is clean and each component has a single responsibility with a clear interface. The abstract bases for `NewsFetcher` and `AgentRuntime` mean you can swap implementations without touching the rest of the system. The implementation order is realistic — you'll have a working loop before you write any fetcher code.

**It's good enough to start. But it needs three additions before you actually start building.**

## What's missing

### 1. Testing strategy

This is the biggest gap. You're going to iterate on every component — fetchers, context templates, agent prompts — and without tests you'll break things silently. Here's what I'd add:

**Unit tests for deterministic components.** The context builder is pure function: JSON in, markdown out. This is trivially testable. Same for staleness checks, task file parsing, and the orchestration logic. These tests are cheap to write and catch 90% of regressions.

**Contract tests for fetchers.** You can't unit test a Google Calendar API call, but you can test that your fetcher *output* matches the expected schema. Write a validator that checks `calendar_state.json` against the spec. Run it after every fetch. This catches API changes and malformed data before it hits the context builder.

**Snapshot tests for agent output.** Pin a known `daily_context.md` as a fixture, run the agent against it, and eyeball the output. You can't assert exact text (LLM output varies), but you can assert structure — has a `## News` section, has a `## Tasks` section, tasks are checkbox format, etc. This catches prompt regressions.

**Integration test: the full loop.** One test that runs `start_day` with fixture data (no real API calls) and verifies a valid daily note comes out. This is your smoke test before every change.

### 2. Future-proofing gaps

The spec is MVP-scoped, which is correct. But a few decisions now will save painful rewrites later:

**No schema validation anywhere.** Right now if `calendar_state.json` has a missing field, the context builder will just crash with a KeyError. Every state file needs a schema (even just a Python dataclass or Pydantic model) that validates on read. This is cheap to add now and prevents cascading failures when you add new fetchers.

**No versioning on state files.** When you change the calendar state schema in month two (and you will — you'll want to add attendees, or recurring event flags), old cached state files will break the context builder. Add a `"schema_version": 1` field to every state file and check it on read.

**The daily note format is hardcoded in the agent prompt.** When you add the Training Coach agent or weekly summaries, you'll want to change the output format. The agent prompt should reference a template spec file (`.system/config/daily_template.md`) rather than having the format baked into the system prompt string.

**No config for the agent itself.** Model name, temperature, max tokens, system prompt path — these should live in `.system/config/agent_config.json`, not as constants in Python files. You'll tune these constantly.

### 3. Developer workflow

You're going to develop this with Claude Code across sessions. The spec should include:

**A `CLAUDE.md`** at the vault root with project context, architecture overview, and the "don't touch" rules (never modify Knowledge files, never write to state files from laptop, etc.). You know how to keep these lean.

**A `Makefile` or `just` file** with common commands: `make test`, `make fetch`, `make start-day`, `make validate-state`. This keeps your Claude Code sessions focused on implementation rather than remembering command invocations.

---

## My recommendation

Before you start Phase 1, add a **Phase 0.5** that takes about half a day:

- Set up `pytest` with a `tests/` folder
- Write the state file schemas as Pydantic models
- Write one contract test per state file (calendar, news)
- Write one context builder test with fixture data
- Write a structural validator for daily note output
- Add `schema_version` to state file specs
- Create `CLAUDE.md` and `Makefile`

Then proceed with Phase 1 as written. Every subsequent phase starts with "write the test for this component" before writing the component itself.

Want me to update the spec with these additions — testing strategy, schemas, config structure, and the revised phase plan? I can produce a v2 that's genuinely start-ready.

### 2. Future-proofing gaps

The spec is MVP-scoped, which is correct. But a few decisions now will save painful rewrites later:

**No schema validation anywhere.** Right now if `calendar_state.json` has a missing field, the context builder will just crash with a KeyError. Every state file needs a schema (even just a Python dataclass or Pydantic model) that validates on read. This is cheap to add now and prevents cascading failures when you add new fetchers.

**No versioning on state files.** When you change the calendar state schema in month two (and you will — you'll want to add attendees, or recurring event flags), old cached state files will break the context builder. Add a `"schema_version": 1` field to every state file and check it on read.

**The daily note format is hardcoded in the agent prompt.** When you add the Training Coach agent or weekly summaries, you'll want to change the output format. The agent prompt should reference a template spec file (`.system/config/daily_template.md`) rather than having the format baked into the system prompt string.

**No config for the agent itself.** Model name, temperature, max tokens, system prompt path — these should live in `.system/config/agent_config.json`, not as constants in Python files. You'll tune these constantly.

### 3. Developer workflow

You're going to develop this with Claude Code across sessions. The spec should include:

**A `CLAUDE.md`** at the vault root with project context, architecture overview, and the "don't touch" rules (never modify Knowledge files, never write to state files from laptop, etc.). You know how to keep these lean.

**A `Makefile` or `just` file** with common commands: `make test`, `make fetch`, `make start-day`, `make validate-state`. This keeps your Claude Code sessions focused on implementation rather than remembering command invocations.

---

## My recommendation

Before you start Phase 1, add a **Phase 0.5** that takes about half a day:

- Set up `pytest` with a `tests/` folder
- Write the state file schemas as Pydantic models
- Write one contract test per state file (calendar, news)
- Write one context builder test with fixture data
- Write a structural validator for daily note output
- Add `schema_version` to state file specs
- Create `CLAUDE.md` and `Makefile`

Then proceed with Phase 1 as written. Every subsequent phase starts with "write the test for this component" before writing the component itself.

Want me to update the spec with these additions — testing strategy, schemas, config structure, and the revised phase plan? I can produce a v2 that's genuinely start-ready.

Here's v2. The major changes from v1:

**Repo/vault separation** is now explicit. `aegis/` is a git repo with all code and tests. `vault/` is Syncthing-synced with only data. This was implied before but not designed — now the folder structures, deploy pattern, and sync boundaries are all specified.

**Pydantic replaces the hand-rolled validator.** The v1 `schemas.py` was a custom dict-checking function. v2 uses proper Pydantic models with `schema_version` fields, so you get type safety, clear error messages, and a migration path when schemas change.

**Testing is layered with a `conftest.py` and `Makefile` targets.** You can run `make test-context` after editing a template, `make test-fetch` after touching a fetcher, or `make test` before any git push. The slow agent tests are marked and skipped by default.

**`CLAUDE.md` is included.** Lean, six sections, fits your style. Lists the invariants that Claude Code sessions must never break.

**Agent prompt lives in an external file** (`.system/config/daily_template.md`), not a Python string. You'll iterate on this in Obsidian far more comfortably than in a code editor.

**Phase 7 (stabilization)** is new — 5 consecutive mornings of useful output before declaring MVP done. That's the real acceptance test.

The spec is now something you can hand to Claude Code on day 1 and start building Phase 1 directly.

1	# Project Aegis — MVP Spec: Daily Intelligence Note
     2	
     3	## Objective
     4	
     5	Every morning, produce a single markdown file `Daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md` containing:
     6	
     7	1. **News Briefing** — AI industry, Anthropic, Boris Cherny (Claude insights/tweets)
     8	2. **Calendar** — Today's events from Google Calendar
     9	3. **Tasks** — Today's todo list (vault-native, markdown-based)
    10	4. **Training** — Summary pulled from existing training plan context (read-only)
    11	
    12	---
    13	
    14	## System Overview
    15	
    16	```
    17	┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    18	│                  VPS (automated)                 │
    19	│                                                  │
    20	│  cron (6:00 AM) ─► fetch_all.py                 │
    21	│                      ├── news_fetcher.py         │
    22	│                      ├── calendar_fetcher.py     │
    23	│                      └── writes to .system/state/│
    24	│                                                  │
    25	│  Syncthing ◄──────────────────────► Laptop/Phone │
    26	└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    27	
    28	┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    29	│              Laptop (user-triggered)             │
    30	│                                                  │
    31	│  start_day ─► context_builder.py                 │
    32	│                  reads .system/state/*            │
    33	│                  reads data/tasks/               │
    34	│                  reads data/training/             │
    35	│                  writes .system/context/          │
    36	│                                                  │
    37	│              ─► agent_daily_planner.py            │
    38	│                  reads .system/context/*          │
    39	│                  writes Daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md       │
    40	└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    41	```
    42	
    43	---
    44	
    45	## Vault Structure (MVP subset)
    46	
    47	```
    48	vault/
    49	  Daily/
    50	    2025-06-15.md              ← generated daily note
    51	
    52	  data/
    53	    tasks/
    54	      inbox.md                 ← quick capture
    55	      today.md                 ← today's tasks (manual + generated)
    56	      backlog.md               ← someday/maybe
    57	    training/
    58	      plan.md                  ← current training plan (maintained separately)
    59	      log.md                   ← recent training log
    60	
    61	  .system/
    62	    state/
    63	      calendar_state.json      ← fetched from Google Calendar
    64	      news_state.json          ← fetched from news sources
    65	    context/
    66	      calendar_context.md      ← human-readable calendar summary
    67	      news_context.md          ← human-readable news briefing
    68	      tasks_context.md         ← assembled from data/tasks/
    69	      training_context.md      ← assembled from data/training/
    70	      daily_context.md         ← merged context for agent
    71	    config/
    72	      interests.json           ← news topics and sources config
    73	    logs/
    74	      fetch.log                ← ingestion logs
    75	      agent.log                ← agent run logs
    76	```
    77	
    78	---
    79	
    80	## Component Specs
    81	
    82	### 1. News Fetcher (`scripts/fetch/news_fetcher.py`)
    83	
    84	**Responsibility:** Fetch news matching configured interests, write structured state.
    85	
    86	**Interface:**
    87	
    88	```python
    89	class NewsFetcher:
    90	    """Abstract base. Swap implementations without changing the pipeline."""
    91	
    92	    def fetch(self, interests: list[str]) -> list[NewsItem]:
    93	        raise NotImplementedError
    94	
    95	@dataclass
    96	class NewsItem:
    97	    title: str
    98	    source: str          # e.g. "twitter", "rss", "newsapi"
    99	    url: str
   100	    summary: str         # 1-3 sentences
   101	    topic: str           # matched interest tag
   102	    published: datetime
   103	    relevance: float     # 0-1, source-specific scoring
   104	```
   105	
   106	**Config** (`.system/config/interests.json`):
   107	
   108	```json
   109	{
   110	  "topics": [
   111	    {
   112	      "name": "AI Industry",
   113	      "keywords": ["artificial intelligence", "LLM", "foundation model"],
   114	      "sources": ["rss", "newsapi"]
   115	    },
   116	    {
   117	      "name": "Anthropic",
   118	      "keywords": ["anthropic", "claude", "claude code"],
   119	      "sources": ["rss", "twitter", "newsapi"]
   120	    },
   121	    {
   122	      "name": "Boris Cherny",
   123	      "keywords": ["boris cherny"],
   124	      "sources": ["twitter"],
   125	      "twitter_handles": ["borischerny"]
   126	    }
   127	  ],
   128	  "max_items_per_topic": 5,
   129	  "max_age_hours": 24
   130	}
   131	```
   132	
   133	**Output** (`.system/state/news_state.json`):
   134	
   135	```json
   136	{
   137	  "fetched_at": "2025-06-15T06:00:00Z",
   138	  "items": [
   139	    {
   140	      "title": "Anthropic releases Claude 4",
   141	      "source": "rss",
   142	      "url": "https://...",
   143	      "summary": "Anthropic announced...",
   144	      "topic": "Anthropic",
   145	      "published": "2025-06-14T18:30:00Z",
   146	      "relevance": 0.95
   147	    }
   148	  ],
   149	  "errors": []
   150	}
   151	```
   152	
   153	**Implementation note:** The fetcher interface is deliberately abstract. For MVP, start with whichever source is easiest to set up (likely RSS via `feedparser` or a news API). Add Twitter/X scraping later. The pipeline doesn't care — it reads `news_state.json` regardless of how it was populated.
   154	
   155	---
   156	
   157	### 2. Calendar Fetcher (`scripts/fetch/calendar_fetcher.py`)
   158	
   159	**Responsibility:** Fetch today's + tomorrow's events from Google Calendar API.
   160	
   161	**Output** (`.system/state/calendar_state.json`):
   162	
   163	```json
   164	{
   165	  "fetched_at": "2025-06-15T06:00:00Z",
   166	  "date": "2025-06-15",
   167	  "events": [
   168	    {
   169	      "title": "Team standup",
   170	      "start": "2025-06-15T09:00:00+03:00",
   171	      "end": "2025-06-15T09:30:00+03:00",
   172	      "location": "Google Meet",
   173	      "calendar": "Work",
   174	      "all_day": false
   175	    }
   176	  ],
   177	  "tomorrow_preview": [
   178	    {
   179	      "title": "Dentist",
   180	      "start": "2025-06-16T14:00:00+03:00",
   181	      "all_day": false
   182	    }
   183	  ]
   184	}
   185	```
   186	
   187	**Auth:** Google Calendar API with OAuth2 service account or saved refresh token. Store credentials in `.system/config/google_credentials.json` (gitignored).
   188	
   189	---
   190	
   191	### 3. Task System
   192	
   193	**No fetcher needed.** Tasks are vault-native markdown files.
   194	
   195	**Format** (`data/tasks/today.md`):
   196	
   197	```markdown
   198	# Today
   199	
   200	- [ ] Review PR for auth module
   201	- [ ] Send status update to team
   202	- [x] Fix calendar sync bug
   203	
   204	# Carried Over
   205	- [ ] Write integration tests (from 2025-06-14)
   206	```
   207	
   208	**Format** (`data/tasks/inbox.md`):
   209	
   210	```markdown
   211	# Inbox
   212	- [ ] Look into Syncthing conflict resolution
   213	- [ ] Research VPS backup options
   214	```
   215	
   216	Tasks are edited by you directly in Obsidian. The context builder reads them as-is. The Daily Planner agent may suggest new tasks (appended to `today.md` marked as `<!-- suggested -->`).
   217	
   218	---
   219	
   220	### 4. Training Context
   221	
   222	**No fetcher needed for MVP.** You maintain `data/training/plan.md` and `data/training/log.md` separately.
   223	
   224	The context builder reads these files and produces a short training summary. The agent uses this for awareness only — no training suggestions in MVP.
   225	
   226	---
   227	
   228	### 5. Context Builder (`scripts/build_context.py`)
   229	
   230	**Responsibility:** Transform state files + vault files into context modules, then merge into a single `daily_context.md` for the agent.
   231	
   232	**Process:**
   233	
   234	```
   235	calendar_state.json  ──► calendar_context.md
   236	news_state.json      ──► news_context.md
   237	data/tasks/*.md      ──► tasks_context.md
   238	data/training/*.md   ──► training_context.md
   239	                          │
   240	                          ▼
   241	                     daily_context.md  (merged)
   242	```
   243	
   244	**Context building is deterministic (Python templates, not AI).** This keeps it fast, free, and predictable.
   245	
   246	**Example template logic for `calendar_context.md`:**
   247	
   248	```python
   249	def build_calendar_context(state: dict) -> str:
   250	    lines = [f"# Calendar — {state['date']}", ""]
   251	    for event in state["events"]:
   252	        time = format_time(event["start"])
   253	        end = format_time(event["end"])
   254	        lines.append(f"- {time}–{end}: {event['title']}")
   255	        if event.get("location"):
   256	            lines.append(f"  Location: {event['location']}")
	< truncated lines 257-781 >
   782	                     self.vault / "data/tasks/today.md")
   783	
   784	    def test_context_built_from_state(self):
   785	        """State files → context modules exist."""
   786	        run_pipeline(self.vault, agent=False)  # skip agent step
   787	        assert (self.vault / ".system/context/daily_context.md").exists()
   788	        content = (self.vault / ".system/context/daily_context.md").read_text()
   789	        assert "# Calendar" in content
   790	        assert "# News" in content
   791	
   792	    def test_stale_state_produces_warning(self):
   793	        """If state is old, context should include a staleness warning."""

   795	        # ...
   796	        run_pipeline(self.vault, agent=False)
   797	        content = (self.vault / ".system/context/daily_context.md").read_text()
   798	        assert "stale" in content.lower() or "outdated" in content.lower()

   800	
   801	### Running Tests
   802	
   803	```bash
   804	# from vault/scripts/
   805	pytest tests/ -v                    # all tests
   806	pytest tests/ -v -k "not agent"     # skip agent output tests (no API needed)
   807	pytest tests/ -v --tb=short         # quick CI-friendly output
   808	```
   809	
   810	**pytest is the only dependency.** No test framework gymnastics.
   811	
   812	### When to Run What
   813	
   814	| Trigger | Tests to run |
   815	|---------|-------------|
   816	| Edit a context template | `test_context_builder.py` |
   817	| Edit a fetcher | `test_fetchers.py` + `test_schemas.py` |
   818	| Edit the agent prompt | `test_agent_output.py` against new output |
   819	| Before merging any change | `pytest tests/ -k "not agent"` (fast, no API) |
   820	| Weekly sanity check | Full `pytest tests/` including agent output |
   821	
   822	---
   823	
   824	## Schema Validation in Production
   825	
   826	Validation isn't just for tests — it runs in the live pipeline.
   827	
   828	```python
   829	# In build_context.py
   830	from schemas import validate_state, CALENDAR_STATE_SCHEMA
   831	
   832	def load_and_validate(state_file: Path, schema: dict) -> dict | None:
   833	    """Load a state file. Return None if missing or invalid."""
   834	    if not state_file.exists():
   835	        log.warning(f"State file missing: {state_file}")
   836	        return None
   837	    try:
   838	        data = json.loads(state_file.read_text())
   839	    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
   840	        log.error(f"Invalid JSON in {state_file}: {e}")
   841	        return None
   842	    errors = validate_state(data, schema)
   843	    if errors:
   844	        log.error(f"Schema validation failed for {state_file}: {errors}")
   845	        return None
   846	    return data
   847	```
   848	
   849	This means a broken fetcher can never crash the context builder — it gracefully degrades to "section unavailable."
   850	
   851	---
   852	
   853	## Configuration Management
   854	
   855	Single source of truth for all config:
   856	
   857	```
   858	.system/config/
   859	  aegis.toml             ← main config (paths, timings, feature flags)
   860	  interests.json         ← news topics (read by fetchers)
   861	  google_credentials.json ← OAuth tokens (gitignored)
   862	```
   863	
   864	**`aegis.toml`:**
   865	
   866	```toml
   867	[vault]
   868	path = "/home/user/vault"
   869	
   870	[fetch]
   871	cron_hour = 6
   872	max_state_age_hours = 2
   873	
   874	[context]
   875	token_budget = 2000
   876	news_max_items = 10
   877	
   878	[agent]
   879	runtime = "claude_api"          # or "claude_code"
   880	model = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
   881	max_tokens = 1500
   882	
   883	[logging]
   884	level = "INFO"
   885	file = ".system/logs/aegis.log"
   886	```
   887	
   888	**All scripts read from `aegis.toml`.** No hardcoded paths, no scattered env vars.
   889	
   890	**Gitignore rules:**
   891	
   892	```
   893	# .gitignore
   894	.system/config/google_credentials.json
   895	.system/config/*.secret
   896	.system/logs/
   897	.system/state/
   898	.system/context/
   899	```
   900	
   901	State and context files are ephemeral (regenerated every run) — no need to version them. Config and scripts are versioned.
   902	
   903	---
   904	
   905	## Script Versioning Strategy
   906	
   907	**Problem:** Syncthing syncs scripts bidirectionally. If you break `build_context.py` on your laptop, it propagates to the VPS.
   908	
   909	**Solution:** Scripts live in a git repo. Syncthing syncs the vault data, but scripts deploy via git.
   910	
   911	```
   912	vault/                     ← Syncthing syncs this
   913	  Daily/
   914	  data/
   915	  .system/
   916	
   917	aegis/                     ← git repo, deployed separately
   918	  scripts/
   919	    fetch/
   920	    tests/
   921	    build_context.py
   922	    agent_daily_planner.py
   923	    runtime.py
   924	    schemas.py
   925	  aegis.toml
   926	  pyproject.toml
   927	  README.md
   928	```
   929	
   930	**Deploy pattern:**
   931	
   932	```bash
   933	# On VPS and laptop
   934	cd ~/aegis && git pull
   935	```
   936	
   937	This means a bad commit can be reverted, and the VPS never runs untested code if you enforce a simple rule: push to git only after `pytest tests/ -k "not agent"` passes.
   938	
   939	---
   940	
   941	## Future-Proofing Checklist
   942	
   943	Things that don't need building now but the architecture must not block:
   944	
   945	| Future feature | What MVP must preserve |
   946	|---------------|----------------------|
   947	| New data source (email, finance) | Fetcher interface + schema pattern is reusable |
   948	| New agent (Training Coach) | Agent runtime interface is source-agnostic |
   949	| Event-driven triggers | State files already have `fetched_at` timestamps |
   950	| Patch proposals | Agent output is already isolated (writes to Daily/ only) |
   951	| SQLite migration | State is JSON today — schemas make migration mechanical |
   952	| Mobile trigger | `start_day` is a script — can be invoked via SSH/Tailscale |
   953	| Multiple users | Config is per-vault, nothing is hardcoded globally |
   954	
   955	The key constraint: **never let any component assume it knows about other components.** Fetchers don't know about agents. The context builder doesn't know about the runtime. The agent doesn't know how its context was assembled. As long as the interfaces (state schemas, context format, agent I/O) are stable, everything behind them can change.
   956	
   957	---
   958	
   959	## Implementation Order (Updated)
   960	
   961	### Phase 1 — Skeleton + Test Fixtures (day 1)
   962	
   963	- [ ] Create vault folder structure
   964	- [ ] Set up `aegis/` git repo with `scripts/` and `tests/`
   965	- [ ] Create fixture files in `tests/fixtures/`
   966	- [ ] Implement `schemas.py` + `test_schemas.py`
   967	- [ ] Write `build_context.py` with hardcoded test data
   968	- [ ] Write `test_context_builder.py` — make it pass
   969	- [ ] Confirm: `pytest tests/` passes with fixture data
   970	
   971	### Phase 2 — Calendar pipeline (day 2-3)
   972	
   973	- [ ] Set up Google Calendar API credentials
   974	- [ ] Implement `calendar_fetcher.py`
   975	- [ ] Write `test_fetchers.py::TestCalendarFetcher`
   976	- [ ] Implement calendar context template in `build_context.py`
   977	- [ ] Confirm: real calendar data flows through, tests pass
   978	
   979	### Phase 3 — News pipeline (day 3-5)
   980	
   981	- [ ] Pick first news source (RSS recommended)
   982	- [ ] Implement `news_fetcher.py` with one concrete source
   983	- [ ] Write `test_fetchers.py::TestRSSNewsFetcher`
   984	- [ ] Implement news context template
   985	- [ ] Confirm: news items flow through, tests pass
   986	
   987	### Phase 4 — Agent integration (day 5-6)
   988	
   989	- [ ] Implement `runtime.py` with chosen runtime
   990	- [ ] Write and tune the Daily Planner system prompt
   991	- [ ] Generate first real daily note
   992	- [ ] Save as `tests/fixtures/sample_daily_note.md`
   993	- [ ] Write `test_agent_output.py` — validate structure
   994	- [ ] Iterate on prompt until structure tests pass consistently
   995	
   996	### Phase 5 — Automation + Hardening (day 7-8)
   997	
   998	- [ ] Set up cron for `fetch_all.py` on VPS
   999	- [ ] Add `aegis.toml` configuration
  1000	- [ ] Add staleness checks to `start_day`
  1001	- [ ] Write `test_pipeline.py` integration test
  1002	- [ ] Set up Syncthing for vault data (not scripts)
  1003	- [ ] Deploy scripts via git to VPS
  1004	- [ ] Run the full morning loop for 3+ days before declaring MVP done
  1005	
  1006	---
  1007	
  1008	## What This Spec Deliberately Excludes
  1009	
  1010	These are in the full Aegis design but **not in MVP**:
  1011	
  1012	- Voice capture pipeline
  1013	- Patch proposal system (no agent writes to critical files yet)
  1014	- Knowledge Distiller / weekly summaries
  1015	- Project Monitor agent
  1016	- Training Coach agent
  1017	- Event-driven architecture (MVP uses cron + manual trigger)
  1018	- SQLite (JSON files are sufficient at this scale)
  1019	
  1020	Each can be added incrementally once the daily loop is stable.
  1021	
  1022	---
  1023	
  1024	## Decision Log
  1025	
  1026	| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
  1027	|----------|--------|-----------|
  1028	| Task storage | Vault-native markdown | No external dependency, edit in Obsidian |
  1029	| Context building | Deterministic templates | Free, fast, predictable |
  1030	| Agent runtime | Abstract interface | Decide later, swap without rewriting pipeline |
  1031	| News sources | Abstract interface | Start with easiest, add sources over time |
  1032	| VPS role | Fetch only | Avoids sync conflicts, agent runs where you are |
  1033	| Daily note conflict | Check-before-write | Simple, prevents accidental overwrites |
  1034	| MVP scope | Daily Planner only | One agent, full pipeline, prove the architecture |
  1035	| Testing | pytest, schema validation in prod | Catches regressions before they break mornings |
  1036	| Script deployment | Git (separate from Syncthing) | Prevents bad code from propagating to VPS |
  1037	| Config | Single aegis.toml | No scattered env vars or hardcoded paths |
